Event Name

Servant of servant

Event Date

2 Jan 1990

Event Location

Mumbai, India

Event Type

Festival

Transcriber

Mansi

Completed

13 Jan 2021

Source Audio

https://audio.iskcondesiretree.com/02_-_ISKCON_Swamis/ISKCON_Swamis_-_R_to_Y/His_Holiness_Radhanath_Swami/Lectures/00_-_Year_wise/Devotional_Nectar_-_1990/1990-001_Servant_Of_Servant_Lecture_Mumbai_-_Radhanath_Swami_1990-01-02_IDesireTree.mp3

e.g.

Verse

NA

Places

Navadwip

Pastimes

Pastimes with Mother Sachi, Lord Chaitanya and Adwaita Acharya

Persons

Lord Chaitanya, Adwaita Acharya, Sachi Mata

Themes

Appearance day of Adwaita Acharya

Lecture Transcription

Today is the most auspicious and sacred day. It is the day of the divine appearance anniversary of Srila Advaita Acharya, one of the divine members of the Panchatattva. It is said in the Caitanya-caritamrita-

panca-tattvatmakam krsnam bhakta-rupa-svarupakam

bhaktavataram bhaktakhyam namami bhakta-saktikam

[CC Adi Chapter 7 Text 6]

[I offer my obeisances unto the Supreme Lord, Krishna, who is non-different from His features as a devotee (Caitanya Mahaprabhu), devotional manifestation (Nityananda Prabhu), devotional incarnation (Advaita Acarya), devotional energy (Gadadhara Pandita) and pure devotee (Srivasa Pandita).]

This means that Lord Sri Krishna, he appeared in this Kali Yuga, the age of quarrel and hypocrisy in five features. The appearance of Lord Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, is prophesized in Srimad Bhagavatam, Mahabharat, and other of the great Vedic literatures. There it describes that in this particular age when the Lord descends, he will descend with his divine associates to propagate the glories of the chanting of the names of God.

kṛṣṇa-varṇaṁ tviṣākṛṣṇaṁ

sāṅgopāṅgāstra-pārṣadam

yajṣaiḥ saṅkīrtana-prāyair

yajanti hi su-medhasaḥ

[Srimad Bhagavatam 11.5.32]

[In the Age of Kali, intelligent persons perform congregational chanting to worship the incarnation of Godhead who constantly sings the names of Kṛṣṇa. Although His complexion is not blackish, He is Kṛṣṇa Himself. He is accompanied by His associates, servants, weapons and confidential companions.]

And amongst his many many associates, there are five principal associates, all of which of whom are direct manifestations of God himself. The supreme Lord Sri Krishna in his original form appeared as Lord Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. His original expansion, Lord Balarama appeared as Lord Nityananda Prabhu. His original incarnation of Karanodakshayi Vishnu or Maha Vishnu appeared as Sri Advaita Acharya. His pleasure potency or Sri Radharani expanded herself as Sri Gadadhara and his marginal energy in its perfect state, Srila Narada Muni, incarnated as Srivasa Thakur. Now, of the Panchatattva, Sri Advaita Acharya, who we are observing his anniversary of his appearance today, was most special and all devotees owe him a great and special honor, because, it is by the power of his devotion that Krishna appeared in age of Kali as Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. You see Advaita Ācārya appeared although he is God himself, he appeared himself as a devotee of God. As did Lord Chaitanya appear as a devotee of God. In the Bhagavad-Gita Krishna explains-

sarva-dharmān parityajya

mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja

ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo

mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ

[BG 18.66]

[Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.]

That we should become his devotees. But how to become his devotees, he came to show by his own example. It is described in the Caitanya-caritamrita that seeing the very fallen and miserable condition of the spiritual condition of humanity, Sri Advaita Acharya, with great devotion appealed to the supreme Lord Krishna to appear in this world. It is described that at the time five hundred years ago, when Sri Advaita Acharya was observing the condition of humanity, he noticed that most religious people were simply attached to the external ritualistic forms of religion, without understanding the real purpose and essence of religion. We find this is very deeply the sad condition of today. People in their homes, they perform their puja, they perform their rituals, they go to the temple, the church, the mosque, the synagogue, and they pray to God and they have so many social forms, which they observe, they give their charity. But the real change of heart, that change of heart which causes love of God, and love, a universal love for all living beings does not take place. In fact, people are more attached and concerned with the external forms and rituals, than they are the very purpose of their religion. The rituals and the religious observances that we perform in and on themselves mean nothing. They are a means; they are a vehicle by which we are to purify our heart and develop love. Love of God and love of all living beings. That is why Krishna concludes the Bhagavad-Gita by explaining

sarva-dharmān parityajya

mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja

ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo

mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ

[BG 18.66]

[Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.]

That you should abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender to God. Without this emotion of surrender, everything is useless. It is also described in Srimad Bhagavatam -

ataḥ pumbhir dvija-śreṣṭhā

varṇāśrama-vibhāgaśaḥ

svanuṣṭhitasya dharmasya

saṁsiddhir hari-toṣaṇam

[Srimad Bhagavatam 1.2.13]

[O best among the twice-born, it is therefore concluded that the highest perfection one can achieve by discharging the duties prescribed for one’s own occupation according to caste divisions and orders of life is to please the Personality of Godhead.]

All your duties in life. ultimately are perfect when they are pleasing to Krishna. Without pleasing Krishna, everything is useless.

dharmaḥ svanuṣṭhitaḥ puṁsāṁ

viṣvaksena-kathāsu yaḥ

notpādayed yadi ratiṁ

śrama eva hi kevalam

[Srimad Bhagavatam 1.2.8]

[The occupational activities a man performs according to his own position are only so much useless labor if they do not provoke attraction for the message of the Personality of Godhead.]

This means that whatever religion you perform or practice in your life and whatever occupation you may accept, if in the end it does not conclude in developing a taste of love for hearing the glories of the Lord and praising his glories, then everything you’ve done in your life, religious or irreligious, it’s all śrama eva hi kevalam, a useless waste of time. So, people have a very sentimental or fanatical approach to religion. Without philosophically understanding, what is the purpose behind it. Religion without philosophy is simply either sentimentalism or fanaticism. We find so many people fighting and arguing about petty little details that differ amongst their religions. That you were baptized by being sprinkled and I was baptized by being dunked, therefore you are going to hell and I am going to heaven. Well, you are dunked, you are going to hell because you have to be sprinkled if you want to be going to heaven. This is literally, I have been in the circles of some of the highest priests in the Christian churches and this is how they talk amongst themselves. They are very much concerned with the details. But they do not understand that whether you are dunked or whether you are sprinkled, the purpose is to purify your heart, give up all your pettiness and surrender to God.

What to speak of in India? My God, if you touch your food with your left hand, you are going to hell for it. What to speaking of dunking or sprinkling? What to speak if you are born outside the boundaries of India, then you have to take your next birth and maybe you’ll be luckier next time. So, religion without philosophy is all sentiment. Sentiment means you perform your rituals, you go to your church or temple, and you do not even know really why you’re really doing it. So many of the young people in India, especially in the colleges, I speak to them, they see their parents they do the puja. They say why are you doing this? Well, your father did it and your grandfather did it, and your great grandfather did it and your great great grandfather did it. This is our family God and we are supposed to do it, but why? Well because we’ll have good fortune and everything will be alright, don’t ask me why, this question? They don’t know why and when they try to explain why, there is such little philosophical substance and people in this day and age who are being taught in universities to this sceptical and to doubt and to scrutinize scientifically, everything, they just think this is all so much superstition, how am I supposed to believe this? It keeps people moral so it has its value, and then philosophy without religion is simply mental speculation. There are so many great philosophers, so many very very fantastic scholars and pandits, who know the scriptures, academically inside and out. But their hearts are still like stone.

Some of them do not even believe what they are reading. I knew one great pandit, he knew the entire Mahabharat by memory. Can you imagine and he knew the entire Srimad Bhagavatam by memory and he knew the entire Ramayan by memory? Such a brilliant scholar, you just ask him, I want to know the verse from the eleventh canto third chapter forty-eighth verse of Srimad Bhagavatam and he will immediately, just without even thinking for more than five seconds, he will recite it from memory. But of course, while he was reciting his verses, he was smoking bidis. Han…between, between each chapter he would light another bidi. I asked him, “why you smoke bidis?” He said, “because I am addicted to them and I can’t give it up.” I said, “well, what about everything you’re reading? Where does it say in the scriptures that you should smoke bidis?” He said, “nowhere in the scriptures it says that I should smoke bidis.” I said, “then, why do you smoke bidis?” He said, “because I don’t follow the scriptures.” He said, “I know them but I don’t follow them.” Well, “why don’t you follow them?” He said, “because I can’t.” I said, “do you have a guru?” He said, “no, I do not have a guru, my guru is my mind and I have such a brilliant mind,” and I agreed, “yes, you have a brilliant mind.” But when your worst enemy is brilliant, he is even a worst enemy. Bhagavad-Gita says-

bandhur ātmātmanas tasya

yenātmaivātmanā jitaḥ

anātmanas tu śatrutve

vartetātmaiva śatru-vat

[BG 6.6]

[For him who has conquered the mind, the mind is the best of friends; but for one who has failed to do so, his mind will remain the greatest enemy.]

That the mind that is uncontrolled is your worst enemy and the more brilliant your enemy is, the more deadly is his influence. Better to just be a humble illiterate and give up smoking bidis and chant Hare Krishna and be happy. So, all this philosophical speculation without understanding the real essence, the real substance, the real goal of religion is ‘śrama eva hi kevalam’ and is useless and that is practically the condition all over the world.

In America, on every block of every city, there is one, two, three, four, five churches. Big big churches with big crosses. Christ was such a great, saintly person and they go into the church and they pray and they come out and then the rest of the block, most of it is restaurants serving meat, bars and prostitution houses. So, they do when they come out of the church. What is the use? And the Muslims, they have their mosques and they come out and so many of them, I cannot generalize and say everyone. But so many of them, they come out and they feel hatred towards someone who is not just like them and there are temples in India, if you are not born the same color, you are not allowed in. Because our God is only for us, not for you. Our God is very select. Although that God that they worship, says in Bhagavad-Gita- Ahaṁ bīja-pradaḥ pitā [BG 14.4] [It should be understood that all species of life, O son of Kuntī, are made possible by birth in this material nature, and that I am the seed-giving father.] That I am the father of all living beings. So, this is the condition.

So Advaita Acharya when he saw that this was the condition of human society, he understood that in this age of Kali yuga -Mandāḥ sumanda-matayo manda-bhāgyāḥ hy upadrutāḥ [Srimad Bhagavatam 1.1.10] [ O learned one, in this iron age of Kali men have but short lives. They are quarrelsome, lazy, misguided, unlucky and, above all, always disturbed.]

People are so unlucky, they are so spiritually lazy, they are so inclined toward sinful activities, that the only means which is practical to uplift the condition of fallen society is to chant the names of God. Therefore, it is said in the Brihad naradiya Purana-

harer nāma harer nāma

harer nāmaiva kevalam

kalau nāsty eva nāsty eva

nāsty eva gatir anyathā

[CC Madhya 6.242]

[In this age of quarrel and hypocrisy, the only means of deliverance is the chanting of the holy names of the Lord. There is no other way. There is no other way. There is no other way.]

That in this age of Kali-yuga ‘chanting the name of Lord, chanting the name of Lord Hari, chanting the name of Lord Hari, there is no other way, no other way, no other way,’ to achieve the supreme perfection of life. It is so simple and by chanting with the proper consciousness, by chanting with the proper understanding of life, we can easily attain the supreme perfection simply by this process. Now when we say chanting the name of Lord, the name of Lord Hari, Lord Hari is the universal God of all religions. Whatever name of the God you call, according to the bona fide scriptures. If you chant that name of God, there is no need to change your religion. That is the means of purifying your heart in this age.

So, Sri Advaita Acharya and his associates, they were feeling great compassion, great sorrow in their hearts to see how people were so proud of their learning and so attached to their superficial rituals and over and above that there were so many people who were just out right atheist because of no scientific tangible information of God. Therefore, people were wasting the valuable opportunity of this human birth. Human life is not meant simply to waste like the animals, making economic arrangements to maintain the body. Such a life is available for the dogs on the street. May be not as sophisticated as you, but they are eating, sleeping, mating, having nice families, defending. Human life is meant to realize God. Human life is meant to transcend the limitations of birth and death and realize your eternal nature and when one attains the human life and does not use it for this purpose, there is nothing more unfortunate and sorrowful than that. Losing all your money, all your fame, all your prestige is not a great loss. But, one moment passing without remembrance of God, that is the greatest loss.

Therefore, the great saints ‘para-dukha-dukhī.’ They are always feeling a very great sense of compassion for those who are not taking the gift that God has given them. So, Sri Advaita Acharya with tears filling his eyes, he was begging people, ‘take the name of Krishna. It is so simple, perfect your life.’ But nobody would accept. Therefore, he began to think, that with all my power, there is nothing, myself or any of the other devotees can do, to convince these people of the necessity of taking to a real spiritual pursuit in life. Therefore, he decided that only Krishna himself has the power to convert them, to enlighten them to the path of Dharma, Sanatan Dharma. So therefore, with great conviction, he went to the bank of the Ganges in the holy village of Shantipur and there he was engaged in very very intense and deep prayer to Lord Hari to descend to this world for the purpose of showing his mercy to fallen humanity and while he was in this meditation, he heard a verse from one of the Vedic literatures, which describes that Lord Sri Krishna is very pleased to fulfil any desire of the devotee that worships him with the water from the Ganges and Tulasi leaves, if that devotee calls out his name very loudly. So therefore, Sri Advaita Acharya, he went to the bank of Ganga and with his Shalagram Shila, form of Lord Hari, he offered the Lord, Ganga jala and Tulasi leaves and very carefully with great devotion decorated the Lord and he would call out with a thundering voice, full of devotion and compassion, he would cry out for Lord Sri Krishna to come to this world and of course as described in Bhagavad-Gita, Krishna said everywhere are my eyes and everywhere are my ears, and everywhere are my hands. He sees everything, he hears everything, he feels everything. So, hearing the call of pure devotion of Sri Advaita Acharya, the Lord, the Supreme Personality of Godhead Sri Krishna descended into this world and appeared in the holy land of Navadvipa Dham as Lord Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and from his very infancy, he was inspiring everyone around him to chant the holy names of God.

Through the wonderful years of Lord Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu’s divine lila, one of his principal most associates was Sri Advaita Acharya. There are innumerable wonderful stories, which describe the pure reciprocation of love that they shared amongst themselves. In fact, Sri Advaita Acarya was older than Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and he was also a disciple of Madhavendra Puri, which made him a God brother of Lord Chaitanya’s guru. So, always showing proper etiquette, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu always worshiped Advaita as his guru. Because according to the Vaishnava principles, one who is an intimate God brother of your spiritual master is also your guru and of course by Advaita Acharya’s most exalted qualities, he displayed the qualities of the supreme guru. So therefore, although all the devotees were worshiping Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and all the devotees were reciprocating love with him in this way and Lord Chaitanya was accepting their devotion, he would never accept Advaita as his servant. But rather he would always accept him as his master and Sri Advaita Acharya did not like this at all. Because he felt himself to be the servant of Sri Chaitanya and Sri Chaitanya considered himself the servant of Sri Advaita and Sri Advaita in the intimacy of his love was always feeling left out, that everyone is taking the dust from the feet of Lord Chaitanya and Lord Chaitanya takes the dust from my feet, this is horrible. Lord Chaitanya is pleased to chastise all other devotees but he will never chastise me, rather he offers me worship and respect. Until he chastises me, I will not feel that he has accepted me as his servant.

So, there are many, many beautiful stories of how Sri Advaita would try to somehow or other take the position of Lord Chaitanya’s menial servant. There is one occasion where it is described, you see, Lord Chaitanya would also sometimes, he would sometimes be in the mood of a devotee and sometimes he would reveal his original mood as God himself. So, one day, in the home of Srivasa Thakur, he was sitting on the throne of Vishnu and all the devotees were approaching him with great humility and the Lord was fulfilling all of their desires. So Srivasa Thakur approached the Lord and he said, “I have one desire, your mother, Sachi Devi, please give her pure love of God. She is worship able by all of us and she is such a soft-hearted and gentle lady in all respects. Benedict her with pure devotion and Lord Chaitanya became very grave, and he said, “mother Sachidevi, I cannot give her pure devotion. She will not attain pure devotion because she has committed an offence to a devotee, ”and Srivasa Thakur says, “how can you say like this? She is your mother, she has taken you within her own womb, she has nursed you, she’s raised you, she’s fed you, she knows nothing, but you and her life. Please, what do we have to do, to appeal to you, to give her love of God?”

Mahaprabhu, he responded that Sachimata has committed an offence to Sri Advaita Ahcarya, who is on the level of My guru and therefore until she gets the dust from the feet of Sri Advaita, there is no possibility of her ever attaining love of God. If one offends my devotee, even God himself cannot give that person love. When Durvasa Muni offended Ambarish, even Vishnu would not give him liberation. He had to atone for the offence by begging the forgiveness and getting the dust from the feet of Ambarish Maharaja. Then he was saved. So, then the devotees called in Advaita Acharya and Srivasa appeal to him. He said, “Advaita Acharya you know that Sacimata has committed an offence to you. We do not know, what the offence is. But somehow or other she cannot attain love of God, until she gets your forgiveness and gets the dust from your feet,” and Advaita Acharya says, “what? The dust from my feet on the head of the mother of the God. This is horrible, this is impossible, I’ll never do it.” Indian technology is always full of surprises. To be a westerner and live in India, you either have to have a good sense of humour or you go insane. So, when Advaita Acharya heard this he was thinking ‘Sachimata, she is the most virtuous, chaste of all women, she is the highest of all devotees. She is the mother of the universe. She non different than mother Yashoda or mother Devaki. She is the most compassionate and devoted of all living beings. She is not an ordinary person. She is on the highest level of pure devotion. Therefore, her feet are worship able by even the demigods like Brahma and Shiva. What to speak of insignificant people like me? I should be touching her feet. It is not possible for me to let her touch my feet’ and then he began to just extoll the glories of Sachimata and he was in such transcendental ecstasy describing the beautiful virtues of this divine mother that he fell unconscious and as soon he fell unconscious the devotees called Sachimata and she very quickly snuck up and she grabbed his feet and took dust and put it on her head and after she took the dust, she became so ecstatic, that I have attained the dust from the feet of Sri Advaita Acharya and she just fell unconscious in ecstasy and the two of them were lying there unconscious, trembling in ecstasy and then they both got up chanting Hari, Hari and Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu was watching all this, smiling and laughing and then when she came to her consciousness again, han Sri… Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, he gave his blessing. ‘Now Sachimata, because she has attained the dust from the feet of Sri Advaita, now she is blessed with love of God at this moment’ and all the devotees became very jubilant. They all began to dance in ecstasy, chanting Hari Hari and then it was explained what offence Sachimata committed to Advaita Acharya.

It is described that Lord Chaitanya’s older brother, whose name was Vishwarupa, and he was an ocean of all godly qualities and he was very beautiful and whoever saw him would be overwhelmed by his charms and his gentle behavior, and his great learning and devotion. So, it is described that one day Jagannath Mishra, the father of Lord Chaitanya and Vishwarupa, he took this young boy Vishwarupa to a meeting of very very learned scholars in the scriptures and during that meeting they asked Vishwarupa which scriptures and which books are you learning from and Vishwarupa was very humble, he said, “I am learning from a little of everything,” and then soon after that the meeting was over and Jagannath Mishra was very angry with Vishwarupa, he said, “why you said like this. Why didn’t you tell them? They asked you a specific question? Why didn’t you tell them which books, and how you are learning, and how you are doing? What is this, a little of everything? Not only did you embarrass yourself, you embarrassed me, you embarrassed everyone.” So Viswarupa was feeling very bad, so he went back to that assembly. He said, “my father is very angry because I did not answer your question. Now ask me some more questions.” So, what have you learned? So, he began to explain some of the principles of grammar and he explained some of the theory of that grammar in the Vedic Scriptures and when they heard this, they were very impressed. They said yes, yes, very good, this is very excellent. How amazingly succinct and correct was your analysis of grammar and then what he did is, he said, “actually you believe what I just said, it’s all wrong,” and then he completely refuted everything he said previously and they listened to that, they were thinking my God, what a brilliant mind, he completely… it, we thought it was correct, he completed defeated, it is all wrong. Yes, what you are saying is very right. You are very learned and very brilliant. Then he said, you think this is right? Of course, it is right. You proved it right. He says, no, it’s all wrong and then through logic, philosophy and all rules, he completely defeated everything he just said said again and then they realized, my God, this person, he is not an ordinary human being. He is the most learned of all learned personalities and Vishwarupa saw that these people were so much wrangling in impersonal and mundane concepts that they had no devotion. Not only were they not willing to chant the names of the God, but they had no sense of bhakti.

So, he went to all the different various schools and academic institutions where people were teaching Vedanta and he saw that nobody had any bhakti or devotion and then he heard that there was a class given by Sri Advaita Acharya. The class was on the book called The Yoga Visistha. Now the Yoga Visistha is an impersonal literature, which refutes the spirit of devotion and Sri Advaita was teaching from the Yoga Visistha and every verse and every word, he was proving the supreme glory of devotion or bhakti and when Vishwarupa heard this he was thinking ‘my God, this man is a paramhamsa. He is a pure bhakta, he is a pure devotee. In his heart there is only love of Krishna and he is constantly engaging in chanting the names of Krishna and worshiping Krishna. So Vishwarupa decided, there is nobody else I want to be with except this man.’ So, from that day on, Vishwarupa, every opportunity he would possibly get he would be at Advaita Acharya’s house and he became the dear most of all loving associates of Sri Advaitva and soon after that, as we know, his parents were about to get him married. He left home and took sanyasa and Sachimata was very very hurt; her heart was aching, throbbing with pain. Every beat, was agony in separation from her son and in her mind, she was thinking ‘it’s all Advaita Acharya’s fault. He was such a nice boy until he started associating with this sadhu Advaita Acharya, he was… after he started associating with him, then my God, he just started becoming completely detached from the world and now he left me and left everything.’ But she knew that Advaita was a great devotee therefore she never spoke a word against him to anyone. Because she knew it would have been an offence. So, she kept it all within and gradually she became very much attracted to her youngest son, Vishwambhar, who is Lord Chaitanya and she was so thrilled with his devotion, with his charm and with his behavior, that her heart just overflowed with the sentiment of love for him and Lord Vishwambhar, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu very much went out of his way to appease his mother from the separation she was feeling from her eldest son and then Lord Chaitanya became very nice boy, very well mannered, very well behaved.

But later on, Lord Chaitanya after he began to congregate hundreds, even thousands of people together to chant the holy names, he was practically, constantly in the association of Advaita Acharya, having Hari Nama kirtan in the street. All day he would be with Advaita and the other devotees in the street doing Harinama kritan and at night, all night he would be at Srivasa Thakur’s house with Srivasa, Advaita, Nityananda doing Kirtan. Even though he was married to Lakshmipriya, Lakshmipriya practically never saw him, his mother practically never saw him and then one day, he took sannyas and after he took sannyas, mother Sachidevi, she considered it’s all Advaita’s fault. He has taken both of my children away. Now I have nothing. Then she just could not control herself, she said Advaita means non-dual. But this Advaita, he is full of duality. In fact, he is completely duplicitous. He appears to be a very gentle, saintly sadhu, but he is actually stealing away these children of mine and tricking them to take to the renounced order of life and because she said like that just one time, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu took it very seriously that she has committed an offence to a great devotee. Even though she is my mother, she cannot be excused from offending a great soul. So, this is the high position that Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu felt towards Sri Advaita Acarya.

It is described, that one time, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, he was having kirtan at the house of Srivasa Thakur and Sri Advaita was filled with love and he began to dance and dance and dance and dance and dance in great happiness, as the holy name was being chanted and he was feeling such separation from Krishna, that tears were flowing from his eyes and his heart was filled with the transcendental ecstasy that appeared like sorrow because he was feeling separation from Krishna and he would dance for hours and hours and the devotees had to stop him and the devotees were so fatigued, but he still wanted to dance more and more. So, the devotees they laid him on the ground where he was just rolling in ecstasy and they went to bathe in the Ganges. At that time, Sri Advaita was alone and Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu came to the courtyard at that time and he wanted to pacify Sri Advaita. He, with all respect, Lord Chaitanya said, that, “how can I serve you?” And Advaitva Acarya said, “I know that you are the supreme Lord Krishna. Just by seeing you, just by being in your presence, all my desires are fulfilled. What more do I need?” And Lord Chaitanya said, “but ask something.” He said, “if you want to offer me a benediction, I want to see exactly what you showed Arjuna on the battle field of Kuruksetra, your universal form.” So then all of a sudden, Advaita looked around and all around him he saw millions of soldiers, half around one side and about half was in the other side and he saw the most auspicious and holy land of Kurukshetra all around him and then he saw riding between the two armies was a beautiful chariot and on that chariot was Lord Sri Krishna, who had a beautiful blackish complexion and lotus eyes and his crown was adorned with a peacock feather and then he looked at Lord Sri Krishna and Krishna’s form expanded into the universal form and within the form of Lord Hari, he saw all the universes and all the planets and all the mountains and all the rivers, and all the demigods and all the human beings, and all species of life. He saw thousands of heads and thousands of eyes and thousands of arms and thousands of legs and then he saw mouths opening and fire pouring from them and all the atheistic, demonic, and sinful people just pouring into that mouth and being burnt and devoured and then he saw Arjuna standing there looking at this form with folded palms in great reverence and when Advaitva Acharya saw this, his limbs were trembling in divine ecstasy. At that time Lord Nityananda Prabhu, who is Lord Balaram himself, he was wondering through the streets of Navadvipa and he could understand, what was happening, that Lord Chaitanya was showing the universal form to Advaita and he did not want to miss this great scene. So, he ran to the house of Srivasa Thakur and he came in to the courtyard and there he bowed down and got up, he saw the universal form and both Advaita and Nityananda were simply watching with folded hands.

In this way Lord Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu in the mood of being the servant of his devotees, was always trying to fulfil all of their desires. Now Sri Advaita was very satisfied, the Lord is giving me so much, I see him dancing every day, I see him chanting, he is embracing me, he is showing me such mercy. But he still has not accepted me as a servant. He still treats me like his guru. He still puts me on a higher position than him. So, one day Lord Chaitanya in the mood of a devotee. You see, a devotee never thinks that he loves Krishna. The more you love Krishna, the more you think you don’t love Krishna. This is the sentimental bhakti and in that feeling that you do not love Krishna, you long for Krishna and that’s a more intimate relationship with Krishna than thinking, oh, I have you and I love you. The gopis were thinking I have no love, if we had any love for Krishna, then how did Krishna leave Vrindavan. The fact that he left us means we have no love for him. So sometimes Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu would be in that mood. So, one time at Srivasa Thakur’s house he was thinking like this and Advaita Acarya, he said to Lord Chaitanya that, “why you are showing your mercy to all living beings, the lowest Jagai and Madhai, killers, thieves, you accept them as your servants and the chandalas, the dog eaters, the people who are claim to be an untouchable class, you are accepting them as your servants and the most puffed up and proud academic scholars who have no devotion at all, you change their hearts and accept them as your servants and all the devotees, even Lord Nityananda, you accept them as your servants. But myself, you’ll not accept me as your servant. You are always calling me guru, you are always calling me master. Why I am so unfortunate? Everyone is getting your mercy except me. If you do not give me your mercy, somehow or other by hook or crook, I will extract your mercy from you.”

Lord Chaitanya did not say anything. He just walked out and Haridas Thakur and Lord Nityananda were following him and Lord Chaitanya was thinking ‘I have no love of Krishna; I have no love of Krishna. What is use of this body?’ And then he jumped in to the Ganges and as soon as he jumped in, Haridas Thakur and Lord Nityananda, who were behind him, they jumped in also and Lord Nityananda took him by the hair and Haridas Thakur took to him by the feet and they brought him to the shore and said, “Lord Chaitanya, what are you doing?” He said, “what is the use of this living? I have no love for Krishna.” We do not understand your ecstasy is one thing. We are completely depending on you. You are our life and soul. Lord Chaitanya says, “do not tell anyone that you have seen me. That is my order to you.” Then he went to the house of Nandan Acharya. He said, “I will be hiding there. No should know I am here.” So, then the devotees, they were all looking everywhere for him. Meanwhile, Nandan acharya was very happy offering him all devotions, giving him nice clothes, he was all wet. Nice dry clothes and putting him nice throne and giving him nice food and giving him nice bhakti. But all the devotees they were looking all around for him and a whole day went by and they were thinking, the Lord has left us and they were feeling very, very sorrowful and Advaita Acharya was thinking it’s all my fault. It’s because I treated him without respect. I said those things on to him that he has left us and he was feeling so dismal in his heart that he just went home without saying anything to anyone and decided to fast.

Lord Chaitanya knew the anxiety, anguish to all the devotees. So, after a day went by, he called Srivas Thakur and when Srivasa came to the house of Nandan acharya and he saw Lord Caitanya, he became very happy, his heart illuminated with joy and peace upon seeing the Lord and Lord Chaitanya said, “how is Advaita?” He said, “Advaita is blaming himself for everything. He thinks you left this world because of him. He is dying, he is about to leave his body in anxiety. Please, you have to give him your darshan, otherwise he will not survive.” So, Lord Chaitanya went to the house of Advaita Acharya and when he went to the house of Advaita Acharya, Advaita Acharya was laying there, tears flowing from his eyes, there was just this …his whole face surrounded by gloom and Lord Chaitanya smiled at him and said, “alright Advaita I have come and Advaitva Acharya just laid there. He could not even look up at the Lord.” He said, “my Lord I have committed such an offence to you. Look what I have done, how impudent, how foolish I am.” And then Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, he said, “Advaita let me tell you a story.” He said, “when a king sends his representative and that representative comes back to report to the King he said, everyone worships that person and comes with all the problems to that person please bring this to the king, please bring this problem to the king. Please tell us what the king says about this. He is given such a high position. But when that same person does something very very wrong then the king’s wrath is most upon him of anybody else. Sometimes he is put to death. So similarly, Advaita, when one is very dear to Krishna, that person can expect that when Krishna’s anger is upon him, it will be the worst. So therefore, Sri Advaita understand that the fact that you have put into the situation, you should not be sad, you should be happy. It is only because you are such an intimate associate of Lord Sri Krishna, an intimate associate of mine that I am punishing you by putting you in this condition and when Advaita Acharya heard this, he understood that ahhhh, Lord Chaitanya has accepted me as a servant. He has punished me and then he became very happy and began to dance.

Of course we must understand that these relationships are the divine lila of the Lord. But there are many many instructive philosophical understandings that can be extracted from them. You see, Advaita Acharya although he was in the highest position, he hated being on a high position. His only aspiration was to be the menial servant of the servant of the servant of the Lord. In fact, all the devotees of Lord Chaitanya, their only aspiration was to be the menial servant of the servant of the servant of the Lord and yet we find in this world, so many yogis, they are performing great tapasya because they want to become God. But the greatest souls, they do not want to become God. They cannot even tolerate the thought of becoming God. They want to simply be the servant of the servant of the servant of the Lord. So many people when they start making a little spiritual advancement, they think oh, I am worship able, I am a very great saint. People should give me the highest regards. But Sri Advaita was teaching us that the great souls, sometimes they have to tolerate accepting worship on behalf of God. But in their hearts, they are simply longing to be the menial servant of the servant of the servant. However great you are, to that extent you want to be the servant and however low you are, to that degree you want to be great, the master. Han? Isn’t that true? In this material world everyone wants the higher position. But in spiritual life everyone wants the lower position. That’s why a lot of people don’t want to come to spiritual life.

I remember, you know devotees whenever they sign off on a letter, they say “Your servant.” When I first became a devotee, I wrote a letter to my parents and I forgot that they do not understand these devotional etiquettes. So, at the end of the letter, after saying everything is alright, chant Hare Krishna, I said your servant and signed my name and my parents became very angry, why you say your servant? After all the schooling we put you through, and after all the education and after all the time and energy, we don’t want you to be a servant! Do you think we spent all this money on your college education, you think we spent all this money raising you and feeding you just so that you could be a servant? Just so that you could call yourself as a servant? Now, stop all this nonsense. Then I realized, they do not understand these signs of bhakti and then in my next letter I was very bold in those days. I was a new devotee. I said, Ok, I am not your servant. I’ll be the servant of the servant of the servant of your servant. I think they understood. It is called the shock treatment. If you put a person in enough, things have to make sense.

So here Sri Advaita who is actually Mahavishnu. Mahavishnu means the greatest of all Vishnus. But when he comes to this world, he is showing how to be a devotee and he is aspiring only to be the loving servant, accepted as the servant. He could not tolerate being worshipped by the Lord and Sachi mata was the Lord’s devotee. He could not tolerate the thought of her touching his feet. But he was always aspiring to collect the particles from the dust of the feet, not only of Sachi mata, not only of Lord Chaitanya, but of all the devotees. So, although these stories seem to be very very, very very elevated and very transcendental, out of the reach of our understanding lilas; actually each of these lilas have a very very practical lesson for us to learn, that the greatest of the great want to be smallest of the small and the smallest of the small want to be greatest of the great. Correct? Therefore, a devotee does not want to be God. He wants to simply be the servant. Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu prayed -

na dhanaṁ na janaṁ na sundarīṁ

kavitāṁ vā jagad-īśa kāmaye

mama janmani janmanīśvare

bhavatād bhaktir ahaitukī tvayi

[Shikshshtakam verse 4]

[O Lord of the universe, I do not desire material wealth, materialistic followers, a beautiful wife or fruitive activities described in flowery language. All I want, life after life, is unmotivated devotional service to You.]

I do not want any amount of wealth; I do not want beautiful woman. Nor do I want followers and disciples to worship me. Nor do I even desire liberation. I have one aspiration that birth after birth after birth, janmani I want to be your unconditional humble servant, my Lord Sri Krishna.

jīvera ‘svarūpa’ haya — kṛṣṇera ‘nitya-dāsa

[CC Madhya 20.108]

[It is the living entity’s constitutional position to be an eternal servant of Kṛṣṇa because he is the marginal energy of Kṛṣṇa and a manifestation simultaneously one with and different from the Lord, like a molecular particle of sunshine or fire. Kṛṣṇa has three varieties of energy.]

My eternal position is to be your servant and the only higher position, than being the servant of the Lord is

gopī-bhartuḥ pada-kamalayor dāsa-dāsānudāsaḥ

[CC Madhya 13.80]

[I am not a brāhmaṇa, I am not a kṣatriya, I am not a vaiśya or a śūdra. Nor am I a brahmacārī, a householder, a vānaprastha or a sannyāsī. I identify Myself only as the servant of the servant of the servant of the lotus feet of Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the maintainer of the gopīs. He is like an ocean of nectar, and He is the cause of universal transcendental bliss. He is always existing with brilliance.]

To be the servant of the servant of the servant of the servant. “ahaituky apratihatā” that is the pure heart of the highest transcendentalist. So, Sri Advaitva Acarya by his life, by his teachings in his most magnificent incarnation, he has demonstrated this quality. Here is the greatest of the great Mahavishnu aspiring to be servant of the servant. Who are you compared to Mahavishnu? Mahavishnu is so big, he is so great. That simply when he exhales hundreds and trillions of universes emanate from his body and in each one of these universe, he expands himself as Garbhodakasayi Vishnu to reside and expansion of his an expansion of his expansion appears within the heart of every living entity ‘Ksirodakasāyi Visnu.’ This is the greatness and glories of Mahavishnu. From his body, millions and millions of universes are coming. In each universe there is so many planets. Each planet there are so many continents, in each continent there are so many countries and cities. Each city there are so many people. Just like this India continent is one little continent on this earth, Bombay is one little part. Khar is one little part, Mahaprabhu’s house is one little part and who are you, you are just one little insect crawling around this one little house. Not even a house, a garage. We each just like, look at just. How big are we even compared to this garage? We are just tiny. What is the size of our body compared to India or compared to Asia, or compared to the earth, or compared to the Universe, or compare to the cosmic manifestation? What to speak of compared to Mahavishnu and each one of us is thinking I am so important, I am God. I am the enjoyer. People should look up to me and respect me for my qualities and Mahavishnu is thinking I want to be the servant of the servant of the servant of the servant. So Advaitva Acharya has given us by his supreme mercy. Not only has he by his supreme love for us, attracted Lord Chaitanya to descend into this world to teach the Yuga Dharma of the chanting of the holy names. But by his own relationship with Lord Sri Chaitanya, he is teaching us what it means to be great? What it means to be humble? Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu has taught us that there is no prayer more glorious than this-

tṛṇād api su-nīcena

taror iva sahiṣṇunā

amāninā māna-dena

kīrtanīyaḥ sadā hariḥ

[Shikshashtakam verse 3]

[One who thinks himself lower than the grass, who is more tolerant than a tree, and who does not expect personal honor yet is always prepared to give all respect to others can very easily always chant the holy name of the Lord.]

That one should be more humble than the grass. Let everyone step on your head. The grass, people step on the head. In India my God, what they do with grass? Specially alongside railway tracks. We have seen, nowhere else in the world has this special feature. What takes place inside of the railway tracks? In every town, city, and village there is a universal pastime that take place. I must say that there is this unity and the nationalism in India in is one regard there is a lot of disunity, but every place is unified by this nationalistic pastime. But the grass just sits there and Hare Krishna. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare/Hare Rama Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. Grass is very tolerant. Lord Chaitanya said you should become more tolerant than the grass. More humble than the grass and more tolerant than the tree. You cut the tree and the tree will give you it’s wood, to build your house, to keep you warm, the tree will give you shade, even though it’s sitting in the sun, burning in the sun, still give you shade. In freezing in the winter, the tree sustaining in freezing and it would give you wood for heat. So tolerant. More humble than the grass, more tolerant than the tree, ready to offer all respect to others and no expect in return. Lord Chaitanya said, in this way you can chant the holy name of the Lord constantly.

Sri Advaitva Acharya by his life example, the greatest of all personalities is teaching us how to live according to the principle of this verse. Actually, to be more humble than grass, more tolerant than tree, offer all respect to others and expect none in return. It sounds impossible. It sounds like a human being just cannot do it. But one who accepts the position of the servant of the servant of the servant of the God, automatically he is doing it. Automatically he is fulfilling this verse. So, let us, on this most auspicious day thank from the core of our hearts to Sri Advaitva Acharya for the great mercy, by his prayer and by his example, by his teachings he has given us and of course the greatest we can glorify him and express our appreciation is to very sincerely and deeply contemplate how we can change our hearts and perfect our lives, by aspiring to live according to this divine principle, of being the servant of the servant and chanting the holy names, in this most sublime state of consciousness. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna!

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
